name: El Ventanón Trail
name_original: Pico Cuerno y el Ventanón — circular por Ermita de San Bernabé
description: Hike the moderate 7.3-mile El Ventanón Trail through the Monumento Natural Ojo Guareña, featuring panoramic views and unique rock formations
region_intro: Hiking in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña offers a diverse landscape characterized by its extensive karst system, including numerous caves and rock formations. The region features dense woodlands, river valleys, and open areas, providing varied terrain for exploration. Trails often follow river courses, lead to waterfalls, and traverse through forested sections. The elevation changes are generally moderate, making the area accessible for different hiking abilities. Best hiking trails in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña The most popular hiking route is El Ventanón Trail, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete, offering views of the natural rock formations. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sendero Cascada de la Salceda - from Quisicedo — circular by LIC (Lugar Interés Comunitario) Ojo Guareña, a difficult 8.1 miles (13.1 km) path, traversing varied terrain including river sections and ascents to viewpoints. Local hikers also love the Salceda Waterfall loop from Río Guareña, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail leading through river valleys and past the Salceda Waterfall, often completed in about 2 hours 45 minutes. Hiking in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña is defined by its river valleys, dense forests, and unique karst landscapes. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña?
A: Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 90 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more demanding treks, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for hiking in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña. The weather is milder, and the natural landscape is particularly vibrant. Summer can be pleasant, especially in forested areas, but it's advisable to start early to avoid the midday heat. Winter offers a unique, tranquil experience, though some trails might be muddy or require appropriate gear.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 120 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the unique karst landscapes, the extensive cave systems, and the serene river valleys. Many appreciate the diverse terrain and the opportunity to explore both dense woodlands and open areas.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña?
A: Yes, Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña has over 20 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These trails typically feature gentle gradients and well-maintained paths, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty without significant effort.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are 10 difficult routes available. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047206899/sendero-cascada-de-la-salceda-from-quisicedo-circular-by-lic-lugar-interes-comunitario-ojo-guarena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Cascada de la Salceda - from Quisicedo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.1 miles (13.1 km) long and traverses varied terrain, including river sections and ascents to viewpoints.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña?
A: Yes, many trails in the region are circular, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salceda Waterfall loop from Río Guareña&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.4-mile (8.7 km) route that takes you through river valleys and past the Salceda Waterfall.

Q: Are there hikes that feature waterfalls in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña?
A: Yes, the region is known for its beautiful waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salceda Waterfall loop from Río Guareña&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, leading directly to the impressive Salceda Waterfall. Another option is the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047206899/sendero-cascada-de-la-salceda-from-quisicedo-circular-by-lic-lugar-interes-comunitario-ojo-guarena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Cascada de la Salceda - from Quisicedo&lt;/a&gt;, which also features the waterfall.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña, provided they are kept on a leash to protect wildlife and respect other hikers. However, access to certain protected areas, caves, or specific attractions might be restricted. Always check local signage or park regulations before heading out with your pet.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña?
A: Yes, many of the easier trails are suitable for families with children. These routes often have shorter distances and less elevation gain, making them enjoyable for all ages. Look for trails that explore the river valleys or lead to accessible natural features.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña is rich in natural and historical landmarks. You can explore the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5679130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Tirso and San Bernabé Caves&lt;/a&gt;, visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Bernabé of Ojo Guareña&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3482674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Cuerno&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails offer opportunities to discover these points of interest.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña?
A: Yes, parking facilities are typically available near the main trailheads and visitor centers within Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña. It's advisable to arrive early during peak season to secure a spot, especially for popular routes. Some smaller, more remote trailheads might have limited parking.

Q: Are there any permits required for hiking in Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña?
A: For general hiking on marked trails, permits are typically not required. However, access to certain sensitive areas, such as specific cave systems or guided tours within the Monumento Natural, may require prior booking or a permit. It's always best to check with the official park information center for the most up-to-date regulations, especially if you plan to explore beyond the main hiking paths.

Q: Can I reach Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña using public transport?
A: Public transport options to Monumento Natural De Ojo Guareña are limited, as is common for many natural parks in rural Spain. While there might be bus services to nearby towns, reaching specific trailheads often requires a car. It's recommended to check local bus schedules in advance or consider carpooling if you don't have private transportation.

faqs.question: Where does the El Ventanón Trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically begins near the Hermitage of San Bernabé of Ojo Guareña. This is a significant landmark in the area and a common starting point. Parking is generally available in the vicinity of the hermitage, which serves as the main access point for exploring the Ojo Guareña Natural Monument.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail, and how difficult is it?
faqs.answer: The El Ventanón Trail offers diverse terrain, ranging from plains with holm oaks to jagged mountains and gentle plateaus where cattle graze. You'll encounter karstic landscapes with numerous chasms (some fenced for safety) and rocky sections. Some parts may have thick vegetation, requiring attention to trail markers. It's categorized as a moderate difficulty hike, with some demanding slopes and ascents. Be prepared for varying ground conditions and a lack of shade in many areas.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail is rich in natural and historical sights. You'll start near the unique Hermitage of San Bernabé of Ojo Guareña, which is built into a cave. The route then leads through holm oak forests and past captivating rock formations. A major highlight is the ascent to Pico del Cuerno, offering panoramic views of the Sotoscueva Valley. The most prominent feature is "The Ventanón" itself, an impressive natural stone bridge often called the "Window of Burgos".

faqs.question: Is the El Ventanón Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, it's important to consider the karstic terrain with chasms and potential livestock on plateaus. Dogs are usually permitted in natural areas like the Ojo Guareña Natural Monument, but it's always best to keep them on a leash for their safety and to protect wildlife and grazing animals. Ensure your dog is comfortable with rocky, uneven paths and bring plenty of water for them, especially on sunny days.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during various seasons, but spring and autumn are often ideal due to milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be hot with limited shade, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended, along with ample sun protection and water. Winter hiking is possible, but be prepared for potentially cold and wet conditions, and check local weather forecasts for snow or ice, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the El Ventanón Trail or the Ojo Guareña Natural Monument?
faqs.answer: Hiking the El Ventanón Trail itself does not typically require a permit or entrance fee. However, the trail is located within the Monumento Natural Ojo Guareña. While access to the natural monument's outdoor areas is generally free, specific guided tours or access to certain caves or facilities within the monument (like the interior of the Hermitage of San Bernabé or the main cave system) may have associated fees or require reservations. It's advisable to check the official Ojo Guareña Natural Monument website for the latest information on guided visits and regulations.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the El Ventanón Trail shares sections with several other routes. Notably, 96% of this route is part of the official "El Ventanón" trail. It also intersects with significant portions of the "Merindades Bike Race 2022" (59%) and smaller segments of "Canales de Dulla" (26%), "Asturian Way" (22%), "Sendero Histórico: Burgos" (22%), "Pico Cuerno" (16%), and "El Valle" (9%).

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring sturdy hiking boots, plenty of water (especially as shade is limited), sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and snacks. A map or GPS device is useful for navigation, as some parts with thick vegetation might make the path less clear. A first-aid kit is always a good idea, and binoculars can enhance your experience of the panoramic views and wildlife.

faqs.question: Is the trail a loop or an out-and-back?
faqs.answer: The El Ventanón Trail, as described in the tour data, is a circular route. This means you will end your hike at or near your starting point, offering a continuous journey through the diverse landscapes without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trailhead is located near the Hermitage of San Bernabé of Ojo Guareña. While this is a significant tourist attraction, facilities like cafes or public restrooms might be limited directly at the trailhead, especially outside of peak season. It's advisable to plan accordingly and use facilities in nearby villages before or after your hike. Always carry out anything you carry in.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through the Ojo Guareña Natural Monument, an area rich in biodiversity. You might spot various bird species, and the presence of cattle on the plateaus indicates a pastoral landscape. While specific wildlife sightings can vary, the diverse habitats, including holm oak forests and rocky areas, support a range of local fauna. Keep an eye out for birds of prey soaring above the mountains and smaller mammals in the wooded sections.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of San Bernabé of Ojo Guareña
